question.text: When two competing open offers are running simultaneously, which of the following rights do target company shareholders have?

answer.text: They must choose one offer and cannot change their decision
They can tender shares to both offers simultaneously for double consideration
They can revise their acceptance between the two offers during the tendering period
They must wait for SEBI to declare one offer as the preferred offer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When competing offers are active, shareholders have the right to revise their acceptance between the two offers during the tendering period. This ensures shareholders can optimize their decision as competing acquirers may revise their offer prices. **The Trap:** "Tender to both offers simultaneously" is incorrect — a shareholder cannot receive double consideration for the same shares. They can revise acceptance (switch between offers) but not accept both. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) tests competing offer dynamics. The shareholder's right to revise acceptance is analogous to bid revision in book-building IPOs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In competing offers, advise clients to wait until close to the tendering deadline before finalizing their acceptance — this maximizes the chance of benefiting from any last-minute price revisions.
